test_suite.details: node test.js reports 3402 passed, 0 failed across all 8 sections (wins.json schema, HTML consistency, internal links, build pipeline, cross-references, sections.json schema, prose integrity, prediction panels).

internal_anchors.details: All href=#X targets resolve (0 broken of 308 unique IDs). All 243 showTab() calls reference a real tab. Tab chain is continuous: overview → evaluate → model → selftest → wins → pages → killshots → timestamp → predictions → falsify → ai → refs.

tab_structure.details: 12 tab-content divs, 12 section-nav blocks forming a complete linear chain with the first prev=null and last next=null as required.

timeouts.detail: connection timed out (retried; likely transient — PO.DAAC has been slow but served the URL in past runs)
TLS handshake timeout on the bare ncei.noaa.gov host; the www.ncei.noaa.gov equivalent path returned 200. Consider standardising on the www. form.

external_links.details: 49 data-source URLs checked (non-citation, non-GitHub, non-dome-site). 43 ok. 2 NOAA URLs initially returned 403 to HEAD with automation UA but returned 200 to GET with a browser UA — not broken. 2 timeouts flagged above, both likely infrastructure flakiness rather than broken links. DOI/publisher links deliberately skipped (Curmudgeon's scope).

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 71 entries (69 numeric IDs + 057b + 058b). docs/index.html contains 71 id="winNNN" anchors — one-to-one match. Verdict tallies: Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4. new_in_v51=32. Build.js ran cleanly against the current docs/index.html (git diff empty after build) — no build drift.

win_detail_consistency.details: Every wins.json entry has a matching id="winNNN" anchor in docs/index.html (including both 057b and 058b). All 71 entries carry tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Detail groups intact (WIN-004/005, 007/022, 008/009, 010/025/068, 011/012, 027/057/069, 040-043/059-061/063, 045/046/049/050/051).

win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. WIN numbers on dome site: 1–69 (contiguous, no gaps). Ours: 1–69 plus 057b/058b as alias entries. PROS-001..PROS-008 aliases present on dome site (expected). No number collisions or title drift detected in a scanned sample.

progressive_disclosure.details: Test suite (Section 7: Prose Content Integrity) validates the ps-*/ks-* TLDR structure and all 71 WIN panels expose tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict. Spot-check OK.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 2 hardcoded light colors in data/sections.json: (1) part1.html — inline style 'background: #f5f5f5' on a monospace block around the g(r,φ) formula. (2) part6.html — inline style 'background:#fff' on a patent thumbnail wrapper around US787412A. Both render as bright white panels against the dark theme. Fix by replacing with var(--card-bg) / var(--accent-muted) and letting the stylesheet handle it.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt present (5470 bytes), contains review URL, mentions dome model. Counts referenced ('69 confirmed') are consistent with the dome site's 69 contiguous WIN numbers. docs/sitemap.xml valid XML with <urlset>, 2 <loc> entries including the main page. docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. <head> has meta description, og:title, og:description, and application/ld+json ClaimReview block. data/uncounted-failures.json has 10 entries, all FAIL-NNN format, all required fields populated, no duplicates.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id=202 > max_id=201 — Phase 0 invariant satisfied, no imminent collision. One ID gap: EXP-052 is missing from the tracker AND not present on disk as monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-052.json. This is a single historical skip (most likely an aborted allocation) rather than lost work, but flagging per the spec. The 174 apparent 'orphan' files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ are archaeological: 76 are plain EXP-NNN.json legacy files whose tracker entries now point to suffixed replacements (e.g., tracker says output_file=EXP-004-sec31-32.json but EXP-004.json still exists from the pre-suffix era), 81 are PRED-assessment-*.json files that the analyst writes outside the EXP pipeline, 8 are ISS-*.json investigation outputs, 2 are MODE5 audits, and 6 are suffixed variants of tracked entries. FUSE cannot unlink, so these files persist indefinitely. None represent at-risk lost work.

workspace_only_files.details: Two completed-work files exist on the workspace but not in git — will be lost if the workspace is recycled. EXP-195 covers four SEC-8b.3 L3a holes from curmudgeon cycle 2 (ISS-1233..1236). WIN-067.c3 is a cycle-3 curmudgeon review. Fix: next workspace-sync run, or manually commit via a clean clone.

section_new_queue_coupling.details: No expansion-tracker items with category section-new or category-proposal-writeup are in status != integrated. Nothing currently requires a curmudgeon re-review gate.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 26 mtime-guard skips in the last 24h across 5 paths (CAT-005.json, PRED-batch-2026-04-18.json, WIN-020.c4.json, WIN-058.c5.json, daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json, EXP-190-proposal.json). All are normal operation — git copies are newer than workspace. No direction-violation skips.

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: Fresh digest regeneration (digest-reviews.js) exit 0. 335 review files → 212 unique base IDs, 337 processed entries, 0 unprocessed, 0 parse errors. Digest reports 204 under-covered processed reviews (holes with no matching open-issues entry, 1117 total missing issues) — this is a pipeline observation for the decider, not an integrity regression.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned fresh from origin/main at /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, copied workspace node_modules in, ran 'node build.js html'. git diff against HEAD is empty — published docs/index.html exactly matches what wins.json + sections.json + generate-html.js produce.

project_documentation.details: CLAUDE.md file paths referenced in prose are accurate for fully-qualified paths. Partial references (e.g., 'analyst.md' → monitor/prompts/analyst.md, 'sections.json' → data/sections.json) resolve correctly via their conventional locations. Schedule table matches the eight scheduled agents and their prompt files on disk. No hardcoded count numbers detected in the rule-of-thumb passages; counts are either supplied as queryable commands or as dome-site-provided values (e.g., '69 confirmed' which is the dome author's claim).

issues_found.description: Completed curmudgeon review WIN-067.c3.json exists on FUSE workspace but is not committed to git. Will be lost on workspace recycle; digest pipeline will not see it reliably until synced.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Let workspace-sync run (next cycle ≤ 4h) or cp to a clean clone and commit manually.

issues_found.description: Completed analyst expansion EXP-195.json exists on FUSE workspace but is not committed to git. Contains four SEC-8b.3 L3a fix directives (ISS-1233..1236). Decider cannot integrate from workspace — must be in git.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Let workspace-sync run (next cycle ≤ 4h) or cp to a clean clone and commit manually.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 is missing from expansion-tracker.json (items jump from EXP-051 directly to EXP-053) and no EXP-052.json exists in monitor/analyst/expansions/. next_id invariant still holds (next_id=202 > max_id=201) so no imminent collision. Most likely an aborted allocation rather than lost work, but worth confirming no work was silently dropped.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Grep git history for 'EXP-052' to confirm it was never a real allocation. If it was, recover from history or mark as intentionally-skipped in a tracker note.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light background '#f5f5f5' in an inline style around the g(r,φ) formula block in part1.html — renders as a bright panel against the dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'background: #f5f5f5' with 'background: var(--card-bg)' and 'border-left: 3px solid #666' with 'border-left: 3px solid var(--accent)'.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light background '#fff' on the US787412A patent thumbnail wrapper in part6.html — renders as a bright white rectangle against the dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'background:#fff' with 'background: var(--card-bg)' or remove the inline background entirely and let the default stylesheet handle it.

issues_found.description: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ timed out on two sequential HEAD/GET attempts. Likely transient PO.DAAC infra slowness — the host has responded normally in previous runs.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Re-check next run. If it persists for three consecutive runs, consider replacing with the specific dataset landing page or a NASA Earthdata alternative.

issues_found.description: https://ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model (no www.) had a TLS handshake timeout. The equivalent https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model returns 200 — standardising on the www. form would eliminate the flake.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json — references to ncei.noaa.gov world-magnetic-model and international-geomagnetic-reference-field

summary: Overall warn. Core site health is solid: test suite passes 3402/3402, build is reproducible (no drift between source and published HTML), all 71 WIN anchors resolve, tab chain is complete, discoverability infrastructure intact, digest pipeline clean, workspace-sync skip log shows only normal mtime-guard activity. Two major findings are workspace-only files at risk of loss: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-195.json — both will be picked up by workspace-sync within 4h but worth mentioning so the decider knows not to integrate EXP-195 until git has it. Moderate findings: one tracker ID gap (EXP-052 missing, no imminent collision) and two hardcoded light-theme colors in sections.json (part1 formula block, part6 patent thumbnail). Minor external-link timeouts on podaac.jpl.nasa.gov and the bare ncei.noaa.gov — likely transient; the www. variant of ncei works.
